payload.answer: The proposal should be repaired before ingestion: it appears to atomize a large table into 500 separate memory cards, which is likely too granular and risks overcommitting durable memory. I’m not committing or merging anything here.
repair_options: Keep the table as a single structured memory item instead of splitting into 500 cards.
Split only rows that are independently durable and user-relevant; leave the rest uncommitted.
Group related rows into a few thematic cards rather than one card per CSV row.
If the rows are additive but low-confidence, present them as user-review choices instead of saved memories.
Trim duplicates and near-duplicates before any memory proposal is generated.
